pattern: A single left-to-right pass with a per-type sliding-window count and a greedy keep-unless-it-overflows rule yields the fewest discards.
hints: Process arrivals in order; the decision for each day only depends on how many of its type are still kept inside the trailing window of w days.
Expire kept arrivals once they fall out of the w-day window, then look at the current type's live count.

Keep the arrival if its type's count in the window is below m, otherwise discard it and tally the discard; greedily keeping whenever allowed is optimal because a kept item only ever blocks later identical items, which would themselves be discarded anyway.

edge_inputs.description: window of one allows one per day
all same type with small limit

stress.description: many arrivals over few types so windows fill up
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    types = rng.randrange(2, 8)
    arrivals = [rng.randrange(1, types + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    w = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    m = rng.randrange(1, w + 1)
    return [arrivals, w, m]
stress.note: few types and a sizable window create frequent overflow decisions
